frameworks.name: signal detection theory
frameworks.one_line: decomposes metacognitive ability into meta-d' efficiency and confidence level

frameworks.name: adaptive feedback training
frameworks.one_line: trial-by-trial feedback targeted at metacognitive judgments rather than task accuracy

frameworks.name: metacognitive calibration (Brier score)
frameworks.one_line: quantifies agreement between confidence and objective accuracy

core_question: Can metacognitive ability be improved by targeted training, and does the improvement generalize across stimuli and task domains?
key_assumptions: metacognition is dissociable from task performance
meta-d'/d' indexes metacognitive efficiency independent of accuracy
Brier score measures calibration of confidence to performance
manipulations: feedback target: metacognitive judgments vs decision performance only
transfer probes: untrained stimuli and untrained recognition-memory task
key_findings: metacognitive-feedback group improved Brier calibration relative to active control
improvements generalized to untrained stimuli and an untrained recognition-memory task
gains driven by changes in both meta-d'/d' efficiency and confidence level
earlier confidence shifts positively mediated later increases in metacognitive efficiency
interpretations: metacognitive ability can be trained independently of task performance
training engages a domain-general metacognitive mechanism transferable across tasks
